quickId: Six eyes in three pairs (most spiders have eight)
Uniform tan legs without banding or spines
Rarely on webs in open view
damageNotes: Bites are uncommon and require pressure against skin. Most suspected bites are misdiagnoses.
management: Glue boards along baseboards in undisturbed storage. Reduce clutter; shake out stored clothing.

quickId: Glossy black with red dorsal spots and a split hourglass below
Small, irregular cobweb in protected outdoor sites
damageNotes: Bites painful and medically significant; rarely fatal in healthy adults.
management: Exclusion, glove use in storage. Web removal disrupts harborage.

quickId: Extremely long, thin legs (often confused with daddy long-legs)
damageNotes: Beneficial — captures other household spiders and insects.
management: Tolerate where possible. Web removal if appearance is a concern.
